title: Cacalia virens
taxonomy: Cacalia virens is classified as a species within the family Compositae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. As a member of the Compositae family, Cacalia virens exhibits characteristic composite flowers. intro: Cacalia virens, also known as Mexican root or Mexican wild lettuce, is a perennial plant belonging to the Asteraceae family, native to Mexico and Central America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The bioactive constituents of Cacalia virens include sesquiterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cacalia virens is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture to treat various health conditions, and is also used in modern herbalism as a dietary supplement and natural remedy.
